competitive.platform: macOS / iPadOS / iOS / visionOS (native Apple-platform suite; Mac is most relevant for a desktop edge-capsule)
competitive.quick_capture:
Two-tier capture, both engineered to require near-zero context switch:

1. MAC GLOBAL QUICK ENTRY (the gold standard for a desktop capsule): global hotkey Ctrl+Space pops a small floating pop-over over whatever app you're in. You type a title, hit Enter, it files to Inbox, pop-over dismisses, you're back in your prior app. Variant Ctrl+Opt+Space = "Quick Entry with Autofill": grabs a link/reference from the foreground app (optimized for Mail/Safari/Finder) and auto-pastes it into the new to-do's notes. The whole loop is 'invoke -> type -> Enter -> gone' with no window management. (culturedcode.com/things/support/articles/2249437)

2. IN-APP MAGIC PLUS: a single floating '+' button (default bottom-right). Tap = new to-do at top of current list; the title field is immediately focused so you just start typing. No form, no required fields — only a name is needed before it's 'ready to go'. (culturedcode.com support 2803582)

Friction level: extremely low. No mandatory metadata, no project picker, no save dialog. Scheduling/tags are OPTIONAL and added via keyboard 'type travel' inside the to-do (e.g. open When popover, type 'tues 8am' or 'in 5 weeks' and natural-language parser resolves the date — supports EN/DE/FR/IT/ES/RU/ZH/JA). Capture and organize are deliberately separated: you dump fast into Inbox, triage later.
competitive.core_model: "Capture now, organize later" GTD funnel expressed as a single vertical spine of time-based lists, NOT folders. The IA top-to-bottom: Inbox (unprocessed staging) -> Today (what you'll start before day ends = your priorities) -> Upcoming (timeline of future start-dates/deadlines) -> Anytime (no blockers, do whenever) -> Someday (maybe later, hidden from Anytime/Upcoming). The key conceptual trick: a 'start date' means 'the day I'll begin', not a calendar appointment — so the system answers 'what should I work on' rather than 'what's on my calendar'. Areas/Projects exist for grouping but are secondary; the time-based lists are the primary lens. The mental model is paper: opening a to-do 'transforms into a clear white piece of paper', details tucked in a corner until needed. (culturedcode.com support 4001304)
standout_interactions: Magic Plus button is draggable and context-sensitive: drop it mid-list to insert at that exact position; drag onto Inbox target (bottom-left) to file there from anywhere; in Upcoming, drop it ON a specific day to assign that start date in one gesture; drag in sidebar to create a Project inside an Area. One control, multiple intents resolved by drop target.
Open-to-card transition: tapping a to-do makes it 'pop out into a card-like form while the rest of the list fades into the background' — depth + focus, reinforcing the paper metaphor.
Haptics designed-in (Taptic Engine): distinct feedback when picking up a task to drag, when interacting with Magic Plus, and when pull-to-search — makes interactions 'feel real'.

Swipe vocabulary: swipe right = open When/reschedule popover; swipe left = enter multi-select; swipe up/down on the selection circles = bulk select/deselect ranges. Long-press = task 'pops out of the list' to drag/reorder without a mode switch.
Magnetic micro-interactions: clickable elements subtly highlight and enlarge on approach/hover, giving a tactile, alive feel.
Type-travel scheduling: inside When/Deadline popovers you type natural language ('tomorrow', 'tues 8am', 'in 35 days') and it resolves instantly — no date-picker scrolling.
Satisfying completion: tap the checkbox to mark done; interactive Home Screen widgets even let you complete without opening the app.
complexity_traps: GTD-complete IA (5 system lists + Areas + Projects + Headings + repeating to-dos + deadlines vs start-dates) — powerful but heavy; a capsule should not import the whole funnel.
Project/Area hierarchy and the sidebar tree — organizational overhead that pulls toward 'manager of lists' rather than 'quick jotter'.
This Evening sub-section, deadlines distinct from start dates, repeating rules — nuanced scheduling semantics most lightweight users never need.
Multi-select bulk operations, tags taxonomy, headings within projects — power-user surface area.
Natural-language parser across 8 languages is delightful but expensive to build; a capsule can ship a tiny subset (today/tomorrow/+Nd) and still feel smart.
Cross-device sync (Things Cloud) and platform-specific gesture sets — scope creep for a single-surface desktop widget.
adopt_for_capsule: Mirror the Mac Quick Entry loop as the capsule's soul: hover/hotkey -> single focused text field -> Enter files it -> auto-collapse back to capsule. Capture must never require choosing a list, date, or project.
Separate capture from organize: everything lands in one default bucket (an Inbox/'unsorted') so the write path stays one keystroke; triage is a separate, optional moment.
Only a name is required — make every other field optional and keyboard-reachable, never a blocking form.
Steal 'Today vs everything else' as the minimal IA: a flat list with at most a 'Today' filter, not the full 5-list spine. Hover-expand shows Today; one tap reveals the rest.
Optional type-travel for dates: let users append 'tomorrow'/'+3d' inline and parse it, but never force a picker.

Bake in tactile feedback: a small spring/scale on the capsule hover-expand, a satisfying checkbox completion animation, subtle haptics if trackpad supports it — 'magnetic highlight on hover' translates directly to a capsule that grows on approach.
Paper/card metaphor for the expanded panel: clean white surface, generous whitespace, details hidden until needed — the capsule collapsed = pen tip, expanded = sheet of paper.
Context-aware capture (the Autofill idea): optionally grab the foreground app/URL as a reference on the new item — a low-cost 'magic' touch for a desktop-resident tool.
lightweight_traits: No required fields — a to-do is valid with just a title.
Single floating '+' as the only persistent capture affordance.
Generous whitespace, custom type for clarity, minimal-but-informative icons — restraint over density.
Details progressively disclosed: metadata 'tucked away in the corner until you need it'.
Optional scheduling — Clear button drops a task back to Anytime (no date), so dates are never mandatory.
Gestures replace chrome: swipe/long-press do the work instead of visible buttons and menus.

https://culturedcode.com/things/support/articles/2803582/ — Using Gestures / Magic Plus button behaviors
https://culturedcode.com/things/support/articles/9780167/ + blog/2017/07/things-3-1 — natural language date parsing (8 languages)
https://www.macstories.net/reviews/things-3-beauty-and-delight-in-a-task-manager/ — animations, haptics, card transition, swipe vocabulary
https://culturedcode.com/things/blog/2023/09/things-big-and-small/ — design philosophy (whitespace, paper metaphor, purposeful transitions)

competitive.platform: Desktop (macOS/Windows native app + web), with iOS/Android parity
competitive.quick_capture: Single-field "one-sentence" capture, zero modal-stepping. The entire task — title, date, time, project, label, priority, assignee, reminder — is typed as one natural-language string into a single input, and a real-time parser strips out the metadata. Trigger symbols are the spine: `#project`, `@label`, `+assignee`, `!reminder` (e.g. !14:00, !30 min before), `/section`, and bare `p1`–`p4` for priority (no symbol). Dates are pure natural language: "tomorrow at 4pm", "every other Tuesday starting Mar 3", "submit invoice Friday", "{march 30}" for a deadline. As you type, recognized tokens get highlighted/underlined inline and lifted out of the title; typing `#` or `@` or `+` pops an autocomplete dropdown of matching projects/labels/people so you never type a full name. Friction level: near-zero — type one line, press Enter, done. Critically there is a DESKTOP GLOBAL SHORTCUT: a system-wide hotkey (customizable in Settings > Desktop > Quick Add global shortcut) opens a small floating Quick Add window from ANY app even when Todoist is minimized; type, Enter, and the window dismisses back to wherever you were. This is the exact "capture without context-switch" behavior an edge capsule wants. Recovery from misparse is one click: tap any highlighted/underlined token to convert it back to plain text so it stays in the title instead of becoming metadata.
competitive.core_model: "Type a sentence, get a structured task." Single text field that is secretly a parser; tasks organized by Project/Section, cross-cut by Labels, ranked p1-p4, surfaced by date in Today/Upcoming/Inbox. Inbox is the frictionless default — capture now, file later (or never).
standout_interactions: Live token highlighting: date/priority/project words underline or pill-ify in real time AS you type, giving instant confidence the parse is right before you hit Enter
Click-to-deselect a parsed token: tap a highlighted word to demote it back to plain title text — instant, reversible misparse recovery with no menu
Symbol-triggered autocomplete dropdown: `#`/`@`/`+` instantly opens a filtered fuzzy-search list of projects/labels/people, so a full name is 2-3 keystrokes
Global hotkey floating window: appears over any app, dismisses on Enter/Esc back to prior focus — the capture surface comes to you, you never travel to the app
Inbox sound / subtle confirmation on add, reinforcing 'it's captured, forget it'
Color-coded priority feedback: p1 red, p2 orange, p3 blue render their color the moment the token is recognized
complexity_traps: Karma/streaks/gamification system — motivation theater, pure bloat for a lightweight capsule
Deep project/sub-project/section nesting hierarchy — heavy organizational overhead that contradicts 'capture fast'
Filters & saved queries with a query language — power-user surface area that 95% never touch
Collaboration layer (assignees, comments, shared projects, activity log) — irrelevant to a personal edge capsule
Boards/Kanban view, calendar layout toggles — multiple competing view paradigms add weight
Templates, reminders-as-paid-tier gating, integrations marketplace — feature sprawl that dilutes the core capture loop
Settings sprawl: customizable Quick Add field actions, productivity reports — configuration burden
adopt_for_capsule: Single-field NLP capture: one text line parsed into structured task — this IS the capsule's hover-to-write surface; keep the parser, ship a tiny symbol set (#, !, p1-3) and natural dates only
The global/edge-summon model: Todoist's global shortcut == our always-resident capsule; capture comes to the user with zero app-switch, Enter commits and it recedes
Live inline highlighting of recognized tokens — cheap, beautiful, and gives parse-confidence; pair with click-to-undo a token (the single best low-cost trust mechanism)
Symbol-triggered autocomplete dropdown on # / @ — fast fuzzy pick instead of typing full names
Inbox-as-default: capture lands in one frictionless bucket, organization is optional and deferred — matches 'simple, not bloated'
Esc-to-dismiss / Enter-to-commit-and-recede as the only two exits from the write state — minimal modal surface
lightweight_traits: Capture is one keystroke (global hotkey) + one line + Enter — no fields, no steps
No mandatory filing: Inbox absorbs everything, structure is opt-in
Metadata via terse symbols means hands never leave the keyboard
Parser does the work silently; UI stays a plain text box visually until tokens light up
Floating quick-add window is small and ephemeral — appears, captures, vanishes

competitive.platform: Cross-platform (iOS, Android, macOS, Windows, Web, browser extension, widgets, watch); freemium with Premium tier
standout_interactions: Inline NLP token highlighting: date/priority/tag/list tokens light up (blue) as you type in Quick Add, with tap-to-revert on any highlighted token — instant visible feedback that the parse worked, plus a one-tap undo.

Fully customizable swipe actions with FOUR distinct gestures per row: Short-Swipe-Right, Long-Swipe-Right, Short-Swipe-Left, Long-Swipe-Left, each bindable to Complete / Due Date / Priority / Move-to / Delete / None. The short-vs-long swipe distinction packs 4 actions into 2 directions.
Checkbox tap = complete with reversible undo (Show Completed keeps it recoverable) — low-stakes, satisfying, animated tick.
Progress on a single task: long-press the task's time, a status bar appears, swipe left/right to set % complete — granular without a separate screen.
Today screen as a calm focused surface (TidBITS: 'focused daily task list') — the default is intentionally narrow.
complexity_traps: Habit tracker — a whole separate sub-app with streaks/calendars; orthogonal to to-dos, adds a second mental model.
Pomodoro / Focus timer with white-noise soundscapes and focus statistics — scope creep into a focus app.
Built-in Calendar with month/week/agenda views + external calendar sync — heavyweight; competes with real calendar apps.
Kanban board view and Timeline/Gantt view per list — power-user PM features that bloat a lightweight todo.
Eisenhower Matrix (4-quadrant) view — yet another visualization mode.
Statistics / Achievement score / 'productivity' gamification — vanity metrics.
Advanced filtering with AND/OR boolean query builder — powerful but a configuration rabbit hole most users never touch.
Collaboration: shared lists, @assignee, comments — multiplayer surface area irrelevant to a personal capsule.
Five+ list types, folders, sub-tasks, custom smart lists — the org taxonomy itself becomes a chore to maintain.
adopt_for_capsule: Single-line Quick Add with inline NLP date parsing as the ONE primary input — for a hover-expand capsule this is perfect: type one line, get back to work. No fields, no modal.
Blue-highlight-token + tap-to-revert pattern: gives the user trust+control over the parser in zero extra UI; ideal for a tiny surface where you can't afford a date picker.
Inbox-as-default-destination: capture never forces categorization. The capsule should accept any text and file it to one default bucket; organizing is a separate, optional moment.
Scope NLP to capture only (not edit) — keeps the parser from fighting inline edits, a clean way to bound complexity.
Reversible completion (tap-to-check with undo) — keep the satisfying tick + easy un-check, since a glanceable capsule needs forgiving interactions.
One calm default view (Today/focused list) with everything else hidden behind progressive disclosure — directly maps to 'collapsed capsule shows minimal, hover/expand reveals more'.
Optional short-vs-long gesture to pack a couple of actions (complete, snooze/defer) into the small expanded panel without buttons cluttering it.
lightweight_traits: Default view is intentionally narrow (Today / focused daily list) — depth is hidden, not absent.
Capture has zero required fields; Inbox absorbs everything so the user never blocks on categorization.
Smart Date Parsing deliberately limited to Quick Add only — a conscious scope cut to protect speed.
Same task surfaces across many filtered views from one flat stream — viewing complexity without storage complexity.
Glanceable checkbox interactions with easy reversal — forgiving, low-stakes.

competitive.platform: Cross-platform (macOS/Windows/Linux desktop, iOS/Android, web) — desktop native feel is the relevant reference
competitive.quick_capture: Multiple low-friction entry points, all designed to capture-before-organize (items land in Inbox/Today, you sort later). Desktop: (1) Global keyboard-driven creation — Cmd+N creates a new task instantly in Inbox/Today; Cmd+K opens a quick-command palette; Cmd+Return opens task detail while creating. (2) Inline hover-add: hovering between rows in a populated list reveals a thin grey insertion line; clicking it drops a new editable task exactly at that position (no modal, no navigation away). (3) Natural-language parsing inside the single text field — type the title plus 'next Thursday', '@person', '#label' and it auto-extracts due date / assignee / label, so one typed line = a fully structured task with zero extra clicks. (4) Voice 'Talk' capture (long-press +, iOS Action Button even when locked, lock-screen widget) transcribes speech and AI-parses it into task/note/list. Friction level: near-zero — the design goal is the thought never has to leave the keyboard or wait for a form.
competitive.core_model: A flat capture-first inbox with progressive organization. Primitives are few and interchangeable (task/subtask/note/list); a list is just an ordered, drag-reorderable stream. Inbox/Today are the dump zones, organization happens later. The model optimizes capture speed and a calm single view over taxonomy.
standout_interactions: Completion as a reward: a satisfying tactile/audio 'tick' sound on marking complete (Cmd+D) — the check is treated as a delightful moment, not a state change
Hand-drawn 'squiggle' / doodle custom dividers instead of plain 1px lines — gives lists hand-made warmth and personality while still organizing content
Hover-reveal grey insertion line between rows — affordance appears only on hover, keeping the resting state clean but making add-anywhere instantly discoverable
Reorder animation: neighboring rows animate aside (~100ms eased) to open a gap as you drag, with the dragged row lifted via subtle shadow/scale — fluid spring-like make-space motion
Smooth view/state transitions ('Task to List switch', reveal interactions) rather than hard cuts — surfaces morph rather than swap
Loading line-path animation and typing-style onboarding motion — motion is used as personality, not just feedback
Real-time voice waveform/transcription during Talk capture — capture feels alive and confirms it heard you
complexity_traps: Multi-surface sprawl: Inbox + Today + Updates + Tasks + Lists + collaboration feeds is a lot of navigation for a single-user edge capsule — a hover capsule needs ONE stream, not five destinations
Real-time collaboration / invite-member / assignee / updates-feed machinery — pure team-tool weight; irrelevant and heavy for a personal capsule
AI voice assistant (Talk) with transcription pipeline — high infra cost and a separate modality; tempting but bloat for a hover-to-type capsule
Custom wallpapers / nature backgrounds / heavy theming — charming but scope creep against 'absolutely not bloated'
Full natural-language parser (@/#/date NLP) — nice but a real engineering rabbit hole; only worth it if dead simple
Notes-as-rich-documents + task/list/note interchangeability — adds editor complexity that fights minimalism
adopt_for_capsule: Hover-reveal thin insertion line as the primary add affordance — maps perfectly to 'hover expands the capsule, then add inline at the exact spot'; clean resting state, add-anywhere on hover
Single-field capture with optional inline NLP (at minimum 'today'/date keyword detection) so one typed line becomes a structured todo without a form
Capture-first, triage-later model: everything you type drops into one flat Inbox stream; no folder decision at capture time
Make completion a rewarding micro-moment: a crisp check animation plus an optional subtle sound — the single highest-leverage 'feels good' detail and cheap to build
~100ms eased make-space reorder: neighbors slide to open a gap, dragged row lifts with shadow/slight scale — the core drag delight
Keyboard-first ergonomics: a global hotkey to summon the capsule + instant Cmd+N-style new-row + Cmd+D-style complete, so power users never touch the mouse
Hand-drawn / doodle divider as a cheap personality signature that keeps it warm without adding UI weight
Motion as identity not decoration: smooth morphs for expand/collapse of the capsule rather than instant show/hide — this is where 'lightweight but premium' is won
lightweight_traits: Praised repeatedly for resisting feature bloat — 'meaningful utility wrapped in a delightfully simple package'
Single-key destinations and minimal chrome; resting UI is calm and uncluttered, affordances appear on hover
Capture flow optimized so a thought is logged in one action/keystroke before it vanishes
Flat list-of-tasks primitive rather than deep project/folder hierarchy
Delight comes from micro-interactions (sound, squiggles, smooth motion) not from feature count

competitive.platform: Amie: macOS/iOS native (Mac menu-bar + mobile, heavy native motion/haptics). Notion Calendar: macOS/Windows/web/iOS/Android, menu-bar + command-bar driven.
competitive.quick_capture:
Two distinct quick-capture philosophies worth contrasting for an edge capsule:

AMIE — capture-as-object-then-place. You create a todo as a first-class card (it lives in an unscheduled "todos" rail, not buried in a list), then DRAG it onto the timeline to give it a time + duration. Scheduling and capturing are decoupled: you can dump a task in 1 second with zero time decision, and only later drag-to-time-block. Mobile uses press-and-hold + drag with continuous haptic feedback so the card "feels held." Friction: very low to capture (just type a title), and the act of scheduling is a physical gesture rather than a form. Reviewers single out "dragging unscheduled tasks into the calendar" and "turning notes into follow-ups" as the signature flow.

NOTION CALENDAR — capture-as-keystroke. The capsule equivalent is its always-resident menu-bar window opened by a GLOBAL shortcut (Ctrl+⌘+K) that works even when the app is backgrounded / you're in another app. The universal command bar (⌘K) accepts natural language ("Meet with…", "Create event…") so a single typed line becomes an event — no fields, no mouse. To-do checkboxes are one keystroke (type `[]`+space, or `/todo`). Friction: near-zero for keyboard users; the global hotkey is the key trait — capture without leaving your current context, exactly the "summon from anywhere" behavior an edge capsule wants.

Combined lesson for the capsule: support BOTH a 1-keystroke summon (Notion's global hotkey → type → done) AND a frictionless "just title it now, place it later" object model (Amie's unscheduled rail), so capture never forces a scheduling decision.
competitive.core_model: Tasks are first-class citizens that occupy the SAME surface as time, not a separate list bolted onto a calendar. Amie blurs the line: a todo and an event are the same primitive — a todo simply hasn't been given a time/duration yet; dragging it onto the timeline "promotes" it into a time block ("connecting events to bigger goals"). Notion Calendar does the inverse fusion: database items / deadlines render inline alongside events and are drag-edited without leaving the calendar. The shared mental model: ONE timeline is the source of truth; an unscheduled task is just a block without coordinates. For the capsule this means: the list and the (eventual) schedule are the same data, and "scheduling" is an optional, reversible gesture, not a mode switch.
standout_interactions: Amie drag-to-schedule: pick up an unscheduled todo and drop it on the timeline to set start+duration in one continuous gesture; the block resizes live as you drag its edges (time-blocking without a form).

Amie continuous haptics: press-hold-drag of a card emits Taptic feedback so it 'feels like holding a small calendar in your hand' — reviewers explicitly call the haptics 'amazing'. Motion is described as 'lively', 'fluid', 'smooth', 'purposeful' rather than flashy.

Amie context-morphing bottom bar: the nav bar's actions change based on what you're doing (e.g. a 'Record Meeting' action appears right after task creation) — affordances surface exactly when relevant instead of cluttering a static toolbar.
Amie onboarding app-icon picker: you choose your app icon during setup — an uncommon 'ownership' delight moment before you've even used the product.
Amie view morphing: smooth animated transition between a daily vertical timeline and a gridded weekly view; date-picking is animated, not a jump-cut.

Notion Calendar global summon: Ctrl+⌘+K pops a compact menu-bar window from anywhere showing upcoming events + database entries in order, with join-meeting/links inline — instant, no app focus-switch.
Notion Calendar natural-language command bar (⌘K): type a sentence, get an event; keyboard-first, mouse-optional, with `?` revealing the full shortcut sheet.
Notion Calendar inline drag-edit of database items directly on the calendar grid — editing a 'task' is just nudging a block.
complexity_traps: Email + AI note-taker + meeting recording/transcription (Amie has pivoted toward 'AI Note Taker' / record-meeting). This is the obesity risk Amie's own founder warned about ('products die from obesity rather than starvation') — a capsule must NOT absorb email, transcription, or AI summarization.

Full calendar surface (week grid, month, multi-calendar merge, free-slot sharing, RSVP, Zoom creation). Notion Calendar's power-user feature density (12 languages, conferencing filters, participant management) is the opposite of a lightweight capsule.
Database/relations modeling (Notion's DB-item-as-event). Powerful but heavy; an edge capsule should store flat todos, not a queryable database with properties/filters/views.
Deep settings surfaces (menu-bar event-count filters, appearance toggles+previews, system-wide shortcut remapping). Useful at scale, but each toggle is weight; ship opinionated defaults.
Treating scheduling as mandatory. The trap is forcing a time on every capture; both apps avoid it (Amie's unscheduled rail), and the capsule must too.
Multi-account / calendar-merge sync plumbing — large surface, not needed for a personal quick-todo capsule.
adopt_for_capsule: Global summon hotkey that works app-unfocused (Notion's Ctrl+⌘+K) → for the capsule: a hotkey or hover that expands the edge capsule from anywhere, type a line, collapse. This is the single most transferable pattern.
Unscheduled-first object model (Amie): capture a bare title instantly; never force a time. 'Place it later' via optional drag is a separate, reversible step — keeps the hover-write flow to one field.
Natural-language single-line entry (Notion ⌘K): one text input parses title (+ optional time later) instead of a multi-field form.

Continuous, restrained haptics/micro-motion on the core gesture (Amie): the expand/collapse of the capsule and the add-todo confirm should have one tasteful spring + (on supported HW) a single subtle tap — 'purposeful, not flashy'. Avoid confetti-everywhere; reserve celebration for genuine completion.
Context-morphing minimal chrome (Amie bottom bar): the collapsed capsule shows almost nothing; relevant actions appear only on hover/expand — affordances on demand, not a permanent toolbar.

Component-first build discipline + ruthless feature stack-ranking (Amie founder): start from a tiny component set, and gate every addition against 'does this add obesity?' — codify a hard no-list (no email, no DB, no AI).
One-keystroke todo checkbox (Notion `[]`+space) and `?`-to-reveal-shortcuts: keep the keyboard path first-class even in a hover UI.
Animated daily↔weekly / collapsed↔expanded transitions (Amie): the capsule's expand should be a smooth spring reveal of the list, not an instant pop — the motion IS the delight.
lightweight_traits: Capture decoupled from scheduling — drop a title in ~1s with no time decision (Amie unscheduled rail).
Always-resident, summonable surface that stays out of the way until called (Notion menu-bar window; Amie's compact native presence) — the Granola-style 'ambient, lightweight' feel.
Keyboard-first single-line entry; mouse optional.
Opinionated minimal chrome — 'see your schedule at a glance', glanceable, low cognitive load.
Native motion/haptics that make a tiny surface feel premium without adding features.
Todos and time on one surface so there's no app/mode switching to plan.

competitive.app: TeuxDeux + Tot (paired study: TeuxDeux = date-list todo; Tot = menu-bar plain-text scratchpad)
competitive.platform: TeuxDeux: web + iOS/Android (PWA). Tot: macOS menu-bar/Dock + iOS/iPadOS/watchOS. Tot is the closest reference for our menu-bar/edge always-resident pattern.
competitive.quick_capture: Tot = the gold standard for an edge/menu-bar capsule: click icon -> panel drops with cursor already in the field -> type -> done (autosave + iCloud, no save/title/new-note step). `tot://` URL scheme appends timestamped lines headless. Only decision is which of 7 dots (active is preselected). TeuxDeux: inline 'click empty line under a day, type, Enter' — the list is the input, markdown renders inline; slightly more friction because you pick a day-column in a full-window app rather than an ambient panel.
competitive.core_model: TeuxDeux: time-axis paper calendar with auto-rollover and undated 'Someday' lists; no projects/tags/priorities. Tot: 7 fixed flat color buffers, no hierarchy or filing — the small fixed count is the feature. For our capsule: fuse Tot's instant ambient buffer with TeuxDeux's date+rollover as the ONLY structure, nothing else.
standout_interactions: Tot dot fill-state as content indicator: the active dot renders as a SOLID filled circle, inactive dots as colored outlines/rings; an empty dot shows NO outline at all — and whitespace doesn't count as content. So at a glance the row of dots tells you which buffers have real text. This is a brilliant zero-chrome status display we should steal for the capsule (e.g. capsule shows a filled vs hollow indicator when there are open todos).

Tot swipe-between-dots with proportional haptics: swiping from dot 1 to dot 4 makes the intermediate dots visually 'fly by' with one haptic tap PER dot passed (3 taps for 1->4). Motion + haptics encode distance traveled — the animation is informative, not decorative.
Tot Dock/menu-bar icon recolors to match the currently active dot's color, and auto-adapts to system light/dark. The launcher itself is a live status surface — the chrome reflects state.

Per-document (not global) toolbar + format toggle: each dot independently remembers Markdown-vs-richtext and whether the word/char-count toolbar is shown; persists across force-quit. Settings are spatially local, so config never becomes a global preferences screen.

TeuxDeux drag-and-drop as the core scheduling verb: you drag an item from a 'Someday' list onto a day-column to schedule it, and drag between days to reschedule. Time-management is direct manipulation, not a date-picker dialog.
TeuxDeux automatic overnight rollover: unchecked items silently move to today, so the list is self-maintaining and you never face a date-picker or 'snooze' UI — the passage of time does the work.
TeuxDeux inline Markdown: typing markdown in a line renders formatting live, so there's no formatting toolbar — the text field is the only surface.
Strike-through completion: checking an item strikes it through in place rather than removing it, preserving the satisfying paper-checklist feel before it rolls/clears.
complexity_traps: Due dates / deadlines (TeuxDeux deliberately OMITS these — argues a due date marks a deadline, not the work, and breeds anxiety; they rely on day-placement + rollover instead).
Subtasks / nesting (omitted — their guidance: if a task needs subtasks it should become its own list, never nested checkboxes).
Per-item notes / rich detail screens (omitted — 'if it needs notes it belongs in a project tool'). No item ever opens a detail view.
Tags, folders, projects, priorities (Tot has literally none — 7 flat dots; TeuxDeux has only time-columns + flat someday lists).
Named files / file browser (Tot drops the entire file-management concept — no save, no naming, no open-dialog; iCloud sync removes the need to ever locate a doc).
Global settings screens (Tot pushes the few settings down to per-dot, avoiding a preferences labyrinth).
Reminders/notifications as core loop (TeuxDeux keeps only light, unintrusive email reminders; no push-notification nagging engine).
Team sharing / collaboration / assignments (both stay single-user; collaboration is the classic bloat vector for 'simple' todo apps).
Unlimited buffers (the temptation to make Tot's dots infinite — the fixed count of 7 is precisely what stops it becoming a notes app).
adopt_for_capsule: Zero-step capture: hovering/opening the capsule must land the cursor in an empty input with NO 'add' button, NO title, NO save — type-and-it's-saved (Tot's 'click, type' standard). This is the single most important pattern.

Use the collapsed capsule itself as a live status indicator (Tot's recoloring icon + filled/hollow dot): the resting capsule should encode 'has open todos / count / nothing' via a tiny fill or color state, so you read status without expanding.
Filled-vs-hollow + whitespace-doesn't-count rule for any multi-buffer affordance — only show an indicator when there's REAL content.
Inline Markdown instead of a formatting toolbar — keep the text field as the only surface.
Auto-rollover of unfinished items as the ONLY time mechanic (no date-picker) — borrow TeuxDeux's silent overnight roll so the list self-maintains.
Direct-manipulation scheduling (drag onto today vs someday) instead of date-picker dialogs, IF we add any time dimension at all.
Strike-through-in-place on complete for the tactile paper-checklist payoff before clearing.
Proportional, informative micro-motion + (on capable platforms) haptics when moving between views/buffers — animation that encodes state, not filler.

Hard cap the structure: adopt a small fixed scope (e.g. Today + Someday, or a tiny fixed number of lanes) à la Tot's 7 dots, and treat the cap as a feature so the capsule can never grow into a project manager.
Continuous autosave + sync with no save affordance and no file/naming concept — the capsule is always 'ready when opened.'
lightweight_traits: No accounts-of-features bloat: both refuse due dates, subtasks, tags, projects (TeuxDeux philosophy explicitly names the 'task labyrinth' it avoids).
No save/file/naming concept in Tot — continuous autosave + iCloud sync means it's 'always ready when opened.'
Minimal chrome: Tot is mostly a text field; toolbar (word/char count) is opt-in per-dot and hidden by default.
Fixed, hard-capped scope (Tot's 7 dots) so it can't sprawl into a filing system.
Settings pushed to per-document scope instead of a global preferences screen.
Single-user, no collaboration/sharing surface.
Self-maintaining list via silent overnight rollover (TeuxDeux) — no manual triage UI.
Plain text / lightweight Markdown rather than a rich document model.
Free, no-subscription, no in-app-purchase (Tot) — minimalism extends to the business model.
Ambient presence (menu bar) rather than a window you manage.

competitive.platform: macOS desktop (menu-bar tray + floating on-screen "nub"/indicator) + iOS companion (lock-screen widget one-tap capture)
competitive.quick_capture: Capture is deliberately near-zero-friction and "write as much or as little as you like." Three concrete entry points: (1) the menu-bar tray icon for open-from-anywhere access; (2) the floating "nub" / live indicator that sits on the right edge of the screen while a session is active — single-click jumps you back into the active note, drag the bottom handle to reposition it; (3) on iOS a lock-screen widget records with a single tap "without fumbling through apps." The note editor itself is a plain markdown surface: type freely with `**bold**`/Cmd+B, `*italic*`/Cmd+I, Cmd+U underline, `#` headers, `-`/`*` bullets, `[]` checkboxes — no modal, no required fields, no save button. Critically, typos/abbreviations/fragments are accepted as-is ("Granola will understand and fix them") and notes are OPTIONAL — the surface never demands completeness. Friction level: extremely low; capture is an afterthought layered on a passive background process, not a gated form. Source-verified: editor accepts incomplete input; no slash-command system documented (markdown shortcuts only).
competitive.core_model: "Stay present, capture passively, refine later." The mental model is a quiet always-on companion that does NOT join/interrupt (it transcribes system audio in the background rather than appearing as a bot). The persistent form factor (tray + edge nub) signals "I'm here and listening but out of your way." User input is treated as low-stakes intent signals ("anything you write is a hint about what matters"), not authoritative structured data — the system tolerates messiness because an enhancement pass cleans it up afterward. Organization is by discrete sessions/notes, surfaced as bite-sized topic-separated summaries you skim, with detail revealed on demand rather than shown upfront.
standout_interactions: Floating edge 'nub'/indicator pinned to the right side of the screen: a tiny ambient presence that is click-to-expand (jump into note) and drag-by-handle to reposition — a persistent affordance that never blocks the underlying app

Progressive disclosure via hover: summaries are collapsed by default; hovering a summary reveals a magnifying-glass that opens a popup with the full underlying transcript context — detail on demand, clean surface by default
Menu-bar tray as a calm home base; movement between notes from tray, system notification, in-app, or the nub — multiple equivalent low-effort access paths
Background-first 'set it and forget it' feel: the indicator quietly shows recording state instead of a heavy active UI; the app 'works quietly in the background until the meeting ends'
Forgiving inline editor: markdown renders as you type, typos auto-tolerated, images drag-droppable inline — typing feels weightless because nothing is validated or required
complexity_traps: Meeting-transcription + system-audio capture engine — heavy, permission-laden, and irrelevant to a plain todo capsule; do NOT replicate the AI-enhancement-of-transcript machinery
Calendar integration + pre-meeting 'Brief' generation — adds account/auth surface and config weight
AI chatbox / ask-questions-of-your-notes layer and auto-generated action items / follow-up email drafting — feature creep that pulls you toward an 'assistant' not a capsule
Multi-surface sync (desktop+mobile+sharing+guest access) — collaboration/sharing is a whole product axis; a personal capsule must resist it
Templates ('Interview', 'Stand-up') and structured note types — even these lightweight templates are scope the capsule doesn't need at v1
adopt_for_capsule: Edge-pinned 'nub' as the core form factor: a tiny always-on capsule docked to a screen edge that NEVER occludes the underlying app — this is the direct ancestor of the right-side hover capsule; keep it click-to-expand + draggable-by-handle

Hover-to-expand as the primary reveal gesture: collapsed pill at rest, hover expands to show/write — exactly the requested model; mirror Granola's 'summary collapsed, detail on hover' progressive disclosure for the todo list

Zero-friction, no-modal capture: typing into a plain forgiving surface with no required fields, no save button, no validation — fragments and typos accepted; this is the soul of 'random capture without interruption'
Quiet/non-interrupting presence as the design north star: ambient indicator over active dialog; the capsule should feel like it's 'out of your way until you reach for it'
Markdown-lite inline formatting for the write surface (- bullets, [] checkboxes) so a todo can be typed naturally without a structured form
Multiple equivalent low-effort entry points (global hotkey/tray-equivalent + the visible capsule) so capture is always one gesture away
lightweight_traits: Lives in chrome the user already has (menu bar) + a single tiny floating element — no persistent window real estate
Optional everything: notes optional, input optional, completeness never enforced
Background-first: minimal active UI, state shown as a small indicator not a panel
Forgiving input: typos/abbreviations tolerated, no validation friction
Collapsed-by-default surfaces with detail on hover keep the resting UI tiny and skimmable
One-gesture capture (single tap/click) from any context

competitive.app: Capsule/Notch-class desktop tools — composite study of NotchNook, Boring Notch (TheBoringNotch, open-source), MediaMate, and Dropover
competitive.platform: macOS (menu-bar/agent app, top-edge notch real estate)
competitive.quick_capture:
No single one of these is a todo app, so quick-capture is reconstructed from their summon mechanics. Two distinct friction models, both sub-second:

1. PROXIMITY/HOVER (NotchNook, Boring Notch, MediaMate): the capsule lives permanently at the screen's top-center notch. Cursor approaching the notch region (NSTrackingArea with .alwaysActive so it fires even when the app isn't key/focused) triggers expansion. NotchNook adds a tactile pre-signal: on hover the notch 'lightly pulses outward' with a drop shadow before fully expanding. No click required to peek; click or two-finger swipe-down commits to the full 'Nook' panel. Friction: zero clicks to view, one gesture to act. Cost: top-center is far from a right-resting hand — a real friction note for your right-edge placement.

2. GESTURE-INVOKED-AT-CURSOR (Dropover): the shelf is NOT always visible — it materializes on demand exactly where the cursor is. Three summon paths ranked by friction: (a) shake the mouse mid-drag — 'give me a shelf right here', single-handed, becomes second-nature/unconscious per Gruber; (b) hold a modifier (Shift/Cmd/Ctrl) while dragging; (c) global hotkey (default Opt+D) or menu-bar click. The shake is the standout: location-based (appears under cursor, not at a fixed edge), single-handed, physically metaphoric.

For an edge todo: the highest-leverage capture is HOVER-TO-PEEK + a single global hotkey that opens the capsule already focused in an empty text field (type-and-Enter to file a todo, Esc to dismiss). Borrow Dropover's 'no-click commit' philosophy and MediaMate's auto-dismiss timer (UI stays N seconds then retracts on its own).
competitive.core_model: A persistent edge 'capsule' that is a SHELF/STAGING SURFACE, not a database. Mental model across all four: the screen edge (notch) is dormant chrome by default and becomes a transient surface on demand. Three shared invariants: (1) idle = nearly invisible / matches system chrome, costs zero attention; (2) expanded = temporary, summoned, and self-retracting; (3) content is short-lived 'in-flight' items (now-playing, files being moved, a HUD reading) rather than a permanent archive. Dropover makes this explicit: a SHELF you stage items on, switch apps/Spaces, then drop elsewhere — the shelf floats on top across Spaces. The right todo analogue: the capsule holds 'today / right-now' actionable items as a hand-off surface; the full backlog lives elsewhere and is NOT what the capsule shows.
standout_interactions: NotchNook hover pre-signal: notch 'lightly pulses outward' + drop shadow on approach, BEFORE full expansion — a 0-cost affordance hint that the surface is alive and reachable.

Fluid expand/contract as one continuous morph (the panel grows from and collapses back into the notch silhouette) — implemented in SwiftUI with matchedGeometryEffect + spring() so the capsule and expanded panel are the same geometric element, not a popover that appears over it.
Dropover mouse-shake-to-summon mid-drag: location-aware, single-handed, metaphoric ('a shelf right here'); reportedly becomes unconscious muscle memory — the gold standard for low-friction summon.

MediaMate slight-expand-on-event: the notch expands just enough to reveal a single control (volume/brightness slider) on the relevant keypress, then auto-retracts on a user-configurable timer — minimal reveal proportional to need.

Boring Notch Metal-accelerated visualizer + album art with color-blend 'magical' effects, and a toggle between 'classic' and 'modern' notch animation styles — animation is treated as a first-class, user-choosable trait.
Multi-touch gesture vocabulary on the capsule (Boring Notch): tap / double-tap / long-press / swipe each bindable to an action — rich input on a tiny target without adding visual chrome.
Drop-onto-notch to instantiate (Dropover + NotchNook tray): dragging a file to the notch spawns a fresh shelf — the edge doubles as a drop target.
complexity_traps: Widget-platform creep: NotchNook accreted calendar, notes, mirror, system indicators, shortcuts, 'live activities' modules — turns a capsule into a mini-OS panel. A todo app must resist becoming a widget host.

HUD/system-replacement scope (MediaMate replaces native volume/brightness/now-playing HUDs; Boring Notch a 'complete macOS HUD replacement') — out of scope and a maintenance sink; do not intercept system events.
Music-control center gravity: Boring Notch's identity collapsed into a music visualizer. A todo capsule should not let any single rich-media feature dominate the surface.

Deep settings sprawl: MediaMate's 'quite advanced' timing/theme settings (4 themes, per-event timing rules) — powerful but a configuration-paralysis trap for a 'simple, never bloated' product. Ship 1-2 opinionated defaults.

Multi-modal summon overload: Dropover exposes 4+ ways to open a shelf (shake, 3 modifier choices, hotkey, menu, drop-on-notch). Each is individually nice but the matrix is cognitive overhead — pick one primary + one fallback.
Notch-hardware coupling: these apps fight the physical notch's top-center position. Don't inherit that constraint; a freely-placed right-edge capsule is strictly better for a right-handed reach.
adopt_for_capsule: Continuous morph identity: make the collapsed capsule and the expanded panel the SAME element via matchedGeometryEffect + spring (response ~0.3-0.4, damping ~0.8) so expand/collapse reads as one organic grow, never a popover.
Hover pre-signal before commit: on cursor approach, do NotchNook's micro-pulse + soft shadow to advertise reachability at zero attention cost; full expand only on deliberate hover-dwell or hotkey.
Two-state discipline: idle (thin, chrome-matching, ~invisible) vs expanded (temporary, self-retracting). Add MediaMate's user-tunable auto-dismiss timer so the surface always returns to dormant.

Type-and-Enter quick capture: global hotkey opens the capsule with an already-focused empty field; Enter files the todo, Esc dismisses — zero-click commit borrowed from Dropover's drag-out-to-finish flow.
Shelf-not-database framing: show only 'right now / today' actionable items as a hand-off surface; keep the full backlog off the capsule. The capsule is a staging shelf, per Dropover's core metaphor.

Reliable hover via AppKit bridge: use NSTrackingArea with .alwaysActive (not bare SwiftUI .onHover, which drops mouse-exit at high cursor velocity and misfires near edges) for trustworthy enter/exit even when unfocused.
Lightweight menu-bar agent architecture: no Dock icon, runs as a background agent like all four — reinforces the 'always there, never in the way' feel.
One primary + one fallback summon only: hover-peek as primary, global hotkey as fallback. Explicitly DON'T ship the 4-way summon matrix.
lightweight_traits: Runs as a background menu-bar/agent utility — no Dock presence, no window-management footprint (all four).
Idle state matches system chrome so it costs zero visual attention until summoned.
Boring Notch is 100% free and open-source — a readable reference implementation in Swift for hover-expand + file shelf.
Metal-accelerated rendering keeps animations smooth without CPU cost (Boring Notch visualizer).
Self-retracting surfaces (MediaMate auto-dismiss timers) mean no manual cleanup / no lingering panels.
Single-purpose summon gestures that become muscle memory (Dropover shake) — low cognitive load.

competitive.app: Edge-Capsule Todo — synthesis of: Granola floating recording pill; Things 3 Quick Entry / Quick Entry with Autofill; NotePlan Quick Capture; macOS notch/Dynamic Island apps (NotchNook, Seam, MediaMate, Bartender Pro Top Shelf); Amazon/Apple hover-intent (hover-triangle) menus
competitive.platform: macOS desktop primary (always-on-top non-activating panel + right screen-edge hot-zone); Windows secondary
competitive.quick_capture:
Two capture paths, both sub-second, zero context-switch.

1) GLOBAL HOTKEY PATH (the TRUE primary — Things 3 / NotePlan model). A non-activating panel (macOS NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel, which sets the kCGSPreventsActivationTagBit so the owning app never activates) summoned by a customizable global hotkey (Things defaults: Ctrl+Space plain, Ctrl+Opt+Space for Autofill). Floats ON TOP of the active app WITHOUT stealing focus or activating your app in the Dock. Type one line, Enter to commit, panel vanishes, underlying app keeps its caret/selection. NotePlan pattern: hotkey -> command bar -> type -> routes straight into a default Inbox note. Friction: ~1 chord to open, 1 line, Enter. Escape dismisses with no side effects AND the keypress is CONSUMED so it doesn't leak to the underlying editor/terminal (cmux PR #847). Things Autofill: panel pre-grabs a link to the current page/email/file so the todo lands with context for free.

2) EDGE-CAPSULE HOVER PATH (Granola floating-pill model, our differentiator). Small always-on-top capsule pinned to the right edge. Hover the edge hot-zone -> expands into a peek panel with today's list + a one-line write field. Glance-and-jot. Critically the hotkey path must remain the FASTEST capture even when the capsule is collapsed — never force users through the hover to write.

Target: capture in under 2 seconds, no app-switch, no window raise, no Dock activation.
competitive.core_model: "Invisible handrail" (Granola's own metaphor): the tool stays out of peripheral vision and out of your workflow until the instant you reach for it, then it is already there — no launch, no window management, no app-switch. The model is a persistent ambient surface + a single Inbox. Everything captured drops into ONE default list with zero up-front triage (NotePlan Inbox, Things Inbox); organizing is a deliberate, separate, later action, never a tax on capture. Granola's retention lesson (cited ~70%): augment, don't replace — keep the user in control, do the heavy lifting (sorting/scheduling) only after the fact. The capsule is a STATUS + ENTRY surface, like Granola's always-visible recording indicator that proves the app is alive without demanding attention.
standout_interactions: Capsule -> panel expansion: spring/ease-out width+height morph (~200-260ms), capsule visually becomes the panel (shared-element / morph, not a separate popover appearing). Mirrors NotchNook/Dynamic Island expand where the pill IS the container.

Hover-reveal with intent timing (NN/g numbers): instant visual feedback <0.1s (capsule brightens/nudges on edge touch), then a 0.3-0.5s dwell before the panel actually expands — this delay is what distinguishes a deliberate hover from a cursor merely passing the edge.
Forgiving dismiss timing: hide only AFTER cursor leaves for >0.5s (NN/g), so micro-overshoots while reaching for the write field don't collapse the panel.

Hover-triangle / mouse-corridor (Tognazzini 1986, Amazon megamenu): while the cursor travels from the edge capsule toward the expanded panel, build a trajectory triangle to the panel corners so a diagonal path doesn't accidentally exit the hot-zone and collapse it.
Edge stickiness on reveal (macOS Dock behavior): when the panel is showing, make the screen edge 'sticky' so the cursor can't slide onto a secondary monitor; release stickiness on dismiss.
Non-activating focus handoff: panel can receive keystrokes and IME input without the underlying app losing its caret; on Enter/Escape, focus returns invisibly (no window-raise flash).
Commit micro-feedback: on Enter, the typed line animates as a small chip flying into the capsule, then capsule shows a brief count badge tick — confirms capture without keeping the panel open.

Ambient idle state: collapsed capsule is low-contrast/translucent and can fade further when the cursor is far away (Granola's stay-out-of-the-way), regaining opacity as the cursor approaches the edge.
complexity_traps: Projects / sub-projects / nested hierarchies / areas — turns a capture pill into a PM tool; kills the lightweight feel.
Per-item metadata at capture time (tags, priority, due-date pickers, assignees) — every field added to the capture box is friction tax. Keep capture to one text line; defer metadata to a later edit.
Multiple lists/inboxes to choose between AT capture — forces a routing decision mid-thought. One default Inbox only.
Real-time sync spinners / account/login walls before first capture — invisible tools must work instantly and locally first.
Rich-text editor, attachments, embeds, AI auto-categorization shown in the peek panel — bloats the panel and slows the morph animation.

Notch dependency: building core UI around the MacBook notch (a real temptation given Dynamic Island apps) fails because not every Mac has a notch and external displays drop it (Apple's own stated reason for not shipping Dynamic Island on macOS). The capsule must be a free-floating edge element, notch-independent.
Hover-only affordance with no keyboard path — NN/g warns hover is undiscoverable/inaccessible; always pair the edge capsule with the global hotkey.
Settings sprawl: dozens of toggles for the capsule. Ship strong defaults (right edge, vertical center, 0.4s reveal delay).
adopt_for_capsule: Non-activating panel (NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel) for BOTH the hotkey capture window and the hover peek panel — never steal focus, never activate in Dock, never raise a window. This is the single most important technical primitive.
Customizable global hotkey as the primary capture path (Things Ctrl+Space), with the edge capsule as the ambient/glanceable secondary path. Don't make hover the only way in.
Consume Escape and commit-on-Enter with full no-side-effect dismiss; Escape must not leak to the underlying app (cmux PR #847).
NN/g hover timing as concrete defaults: 0.1s visual feedback, 0.3-0.5s dwell-to-expand, >0.5s leave-to-collapse — tunable but ship these.
Hover-triangle / corridor + sticky-edge to make the edge-to-panel travel forgiving and prevent accidental collapse and cursor sliding to a second monitor (the documented multi-monitor failure mode).
Single Inbox capture model (NotePlan/Things) — capture writes one line to a default list, triage is a separate deliberate act.
Things 'Autofill' context grab — optionally attach the link/title of the frontmost app/page to the captured todo, so capture-with-context costs zero extra taps.
Granola 'invisible handrail' idle behavior — collapsed capsule is translucent/low-contrast and fades when the cursor is far, brightening on approach; status presence without attention-grabbing.
Pill-IS-the-container morph animation (Dynamic Island/NotchNook) for the expand, ~200-260ms spring, so the capsule and panel feel like one continuous object.
lightweight_traits: Zero-launch: always running, capture is instant — no cold start, no window to find (Granola one-tap-already-recording feel)
One-line capture, one default Inbox, no required fields
Local-first / works offline before any account
Stays out of peripheral vision until summoned (translucent idle capsule)
Strong opinionated defaults over configuration
No app-switch and no Dock activation for any capture

feasibility.question: Can Tauri 2.x build a right-edge-docked, always-on-top, transparent frameless capsule window that is click-through when idle, positionable to the screen edge, persists after app minimize, and is lightweight with good animation? Which exact APIs/plugins/config are needed, and what are the limits?
findings: Every individual requirement maps to a real Tauri 2 config flag or API. In tauri.conf.json the window object supports `transparent: true`, `decorations: false`, `alwaysOnTop: true`, `skipTaskbar: true`, plus sizing/position keys — confirmed in the official Configuration and Window Customization docs. These can also be set programmatically via the Rust WebviewWindowBuilder (`.transparent()`, `.decorations()`, `.always_on_top()`, `.skip_taskbar()`).

macOS transparency REQUIRES `app.macOSPrivateApi: true` in tauri.conf.json (also surfaced as the `macos-private-api` Cargo feature). Per official docs this enables the transparent-background API and sets fullScreenEnabled. Hard consequence: an app using this in production CANNOT be submitted to the Mac App Store (notarization + direct/DMG distribution still work). This is the single biggest constraint and is non-negotiable for a transparent capsule on macOS.

Click-through (idle pass-through) is supported via `WebviewWindow.setIgnoreCursorEvents(true)` (JS) / `window.set_ignore_cursor_events(true)` (Rust). BUT Tauri/wry has NO per-region hit-testing: setting it true makes the ENTIRE window click-through, including your visible capsule pixels. There is an open feature request (tauri issue #13070) and known non-working reports (#11461, #6164). The established workaround is a Rust/JS loop that polls cursor position (~60fps) against the capsule's screen rect and toggles setIgnoreCursorEvents — exactly what a production Tauri-v2 overlay author documented. For your design this is actually clean: idle state = ignore=true (full pass-through); on cursor entering the edge hot-zone, flip ignore=false and expand. You drive the toggle from the polling loop or from monitoring global cursor position rather than relying on DOM hover (DOM events don't fire while ignore=true).

Edge positioning is fully supported: `availableMonitors()`/`currentMonitor()` give monitor position+size+scaleFactor; `outerSize()` gives the window size; compute x = monitor.position.x + monitor.size.width - window.width and call `setPosition(new PhysicalPosition(x, y))`. Use Physical (not Logical) coordinates and account for scaleFactor on Retina to avoid the multi-monitor offset bugs (tauri #7890). Re-run on monitor-change/resize events to stay pinned to the right edge.

Persist-after-minimize is a standard menubar/overlay pattern: set `tauri::ActivationPolicy::Accessory` (config `app.macOSPrivateApi`-independent; via `app.set_activation_policy(ActivationPolicy::Accessory)` or `"activationPolicy": "accessory"`) so the capsule has no Dock/Cmd-Tab presence and is not tied to a 'main' app window's minimize lifecycle. The capsule is its own always-on-top window; the 'main' app minimizing does not hide it. Pair with the official tray-icon plugin (`TrayIconBuilder`) for a menubar anchor and `tauri-plugin-global-shortcut` (`app.global_shortcut().register(...)`) for a quick-add hotkey.

Multi-window: create the capsule as a dedicated WebviewWindow (second window) distinct from any settings/main window — define it in `app.windows[]` in config or build at runtime with WebviewWindowBuilder. This isolates the transparent+click-through window from normal app windows so the polling/ignore-cursor logic only affects the capsule.

Footprint is genuinely lightweight (uses the OS WebView2/WKWebView, no bundled Chromium). A real Tauri-v2 overlay reported ~14 MB RAM / <1% CPU on Windows 11 and ~20-29 MB on macOS Sequoia. Caveat: the same author measured macOS RAM climbing on newer OS versions — ~66 MB on Sonoma and ~110 MB (15.6% CPU peak) on Tahoe — driven by WKWebView/compositor, not Tauri itself. Still far below Electron, but budget 30-110 MB on macOS depending on OS version, and profile the 60fps cursor-poll loop (move it to a timer of ~30-60ms and short-circuit when state is unchanged to keep CPU near idle).

Animation: hover-to-expand capsule animation runs as normal CSS/JS inside the webview (transform/opacity transitions on the panel; animate the window itself by also resizing via `setSize` if the panel must grow beyond initial bounds). Note the macOS gotcha below re: backdrop-filter.
apis_or_mechanisms: tauri.conf.json window: transparent:true, decorations:false, alwaysOnTop:true, skipTaskbar:true, width/height, x/y, resizable:false
tauri.conf.json app.macOSPrivateApi:true (REQUIRED for macOS transparency; blocks Mac App Store)
Cargo feature: tauri = { features = ["macos-private-api"] }
WebviewWindowBuilder (Rust): .transparent() .decorations(false) .always_on_top(true) .skip_taskbar(true) .build()
setIgnoreCursorEvents(bool) (JS) / window.set_ignore_cursor_events(bool) (Rust) — whole-window only, no per-region hit-test
availableMonitors() / currentMonitor() / outerSize() / setPosition(PhysicalPosition) / setSize() for edge-dock + expand
tauri::ActivationPolicy::Accessory via app.set_activation_policy(...) or app.activationPolicy config — hide Dock/Cmd-Tab, decouple from minimize
tauri-plugin-global-shortcut: app.global_shortcut().register(shortcut, handler) for quick-add hotkey
tray-icon: tauri::tray::TrayIconBuilder / TrayIconEvent for menubar anchor
data-tauri-drag-region attribute + startDragging() if the capsule must be draggable
Cursor-position polling loop (Rust/JS ~30-60ms) toggling ignore_cursor_events to simulate per-region click-through (idle=ignore on)
gotchas: macOSPrivateApi permanently disqualifies the app from the Mac App Store. If MAS distribution is ever required, transparent windows are off the table on macOS — only direct/DMG/notarized distribution works.

No native per-region click-through: setIgnoreCursorEvents toggles the WHOLE window. While idle/ignore=true, DOM mouse/hover events do NOT fire, so the 'hover edge to expand' trigger must come from a cursor-position poll (or global pointer monitor), not from CSS :hover. Plan the state machine: idle(ignore=true) -> cursor enters edge rect -> ignore=false + expand -> cursor leaves panel -> collapse + ignore=true.
CSS backdrop-filter: blur() is broken on macOS transparent Tauri windows — frosted-glass capsule effect won't work; use solid/semi-opaque fills or native NSVisualEffectView via objc2-app-kit instead.

macOS isolates fullscreen apps: an always-on-top overlay may NOT float over other apps' fullscreen spaces by default (tauri #11488). Needs visibleOnAllWorkspaces and possibly a private/third-party NSWindow collectionBehavior tweak; verify against your 'always visible' requirement.

Multi-monitor positioning bugs: physical position/monitor coords can be reported incorrectly (tauri #7890); always use PhysicalPosition and multiply by scaleFactor, and recompute on monitor/resize events.
macOS memory/CPU regression on newer OS (Sonoma ~66MB, Tahoe ~110MB / 15.6% CPU peak) — driven by WKWebView, not Tauri. Throttle the cursor-poll loop and verify it idles near 0% CPU when collapsed.
Transparency can be lost specifically in packaged DMG builds even when it works in dev (tauri #13415) — test the bundled .app, not just `tauri dev`.
set_activation_policy at runtime has historically interfered with window.show() (tauri #5122, #9244) — set Accessory at setup/startup and test show/hide of the capsule.
On Windows, decorations:false + shadow can still leak a title bar in some cases (tauri #14859) — set shadow:false and verify.
feasibility.recommendation: Feasible and a good fit — build the capsule as a dedicated transparent/alwaysOnTop/skipTaskbar WebviewWindow with macOSPrivateApi + ActivationPolicy::Accessory, and drive idle-click-through via a throttled cursor-position poll that toggles setIgnoreCursorEvents (idle=on, expand=off), pinned to the right edge via currentMonitor()+setPosition; just accept up front that macOS transparency means no Mac App Store, no backdrop-filter blur, and that hover-expand must be cursor-poll-driven rather than CSS :hover.

feasibility.question: Electron 能否实现边缘常驻胶囊(transparent/frameless/alwaysOnTop/skipTaskbar/setIgnoreMouseEvents/globalShortcut/Tray)？与 Tauri 的取舍。
findings: 全部七项能力 Electron 都有官方一等 API，无需第三方 hack：①frameless → BrowserWindow 构造项 frame:false；②透明 → transparent:true；③置顶 → alwaysOnTop:true（构造项）+ win.setAlwaysOnTop(flag, level, relativeLevel)，level 可设 'screen-saver'/'floating' 把胶囊压在系统级层；④不进任务栏/Dock → skipTaskbar:true（Win/Linux），macOS 隐藏 Dock 用 app.dock.hide() 或 LSUIElement；⑤鼠标穿透 → win.setIgnoreMouseEvents(true, {forward:true})，forward 让 idle 态胶囊点击穿透但仍能收到 mousemove/mouseenter 触发热区展开。

胶囊'软件最小化后仍常驻'天然满足：Electron 主进程与渲染窗口生命周期独立，主窗 hide()/最小化不影响这个独立的胶囊 BrowserWindow——它本就是一个常驻 always-on-top 窗口，不依赖主窗存活。配合 Tray(systemTray) 提供退出/显隐入口，globalShortcut.register('Cmd+Shift+T') 提供全局唤起，二者均为官方模块。

'idle 极小 → hover 展开成面板'的动画用 win.setBounds(bounds, true)（第二参 animate=true，macOS 原生缓动）或在渲染层用 CSS/JS 动画把窗口做大、内容用 transform/opacity 过渡（推荐窗口预留展开后尺寸+内部 CSS 动画，避免逐帧 setBounds 卡顿）。setIgnoreMouseEvents 动态切换：idle 时 (true,{forward:true}) 让大部分透明区域穿透、只有边缘热区响应；展开后 setIgnoreMouseEvents(false) 让面板可交互。

贴边定位用 screen 模块 getDisplayNearestPoint / getPrimaryDisplay().workArea 计算右侧边缘坐标，win.setBounds 贴住 workArea.x+workArea.width-capsuleWidth；多屏/分辨率变化监听 screen 的 'display-metrics-changed' 重新吸附。setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces(true,{visibleOnFullScreen:true}) 让胶囊跨所有 Space 常驻（macOS/Linux）。
Tauri 2.0 同样具备全部能力（decorations:false、transparent、always_on_top、skip_taskbar、set_ignore_cursor_events、global-shortcut 插件、tray-icon），所以可行性两者都成立；差异在工程取舍而非能力有无。

体积/内存取舍（官方+实测博客）：Electron 安装包 80–150MB、idle 内存约 150–300MB（每个 BrowserWindow 一个 Chromium 渲染进程，胶囊+主窗=多进程叠加）；Tauri 安装包 <10MB、idle 约 30–50MB（复用系统 WebView：macOS WKWebView / Win WebView2 / Linux WebKitGTK）。对一个'要轻量、常驻后台、内存敏感'的胶囊类工具，Tauri 在体积和常驻内存上明显占优。
原生感/一致性取舍：Electron 自带 Chromium，跨平台渲染（含透明/vibrancy/动画）行为一致、调试简单、动效更可控；Tauri 用各平台原生 WebView，CSS/字体/透明效果在 WKWebView 与 WebView2 间有渲染差异，复杂动画与透明叠层需各平台单测。对'美观+动效好+透明胶囊'这种视觉/动画密集场景，Electron 的渲染一致性反而是优势。
gotchas: 透明窗 + vibrancy 在 macOS 有长期已知 bug：window 随机变白底（issue #31862/#25547）、切换 app 后丢失 vibrancy、loadURL/刷新后出现白底（#20357）。若胶囊要毛玻璃质感，需谨慎测试，必要时退回纯透明 + CSS 自绘背景。
透明窗官方限制：macOS 上透明窗不显示原生窗口阴影（胶囊阴影得自己用 CSS box-shadow 画）；部分平台 transparent 窗设 resizable:true 会让透明失效——胶囊建议 resizable:false，靠 setBounds 程序化改尺寸做展开动画。
显隐切换透明窗有 fade-in 闪烁（issue #12130/#10069），首次 show 或 toggle 可见性时易见 flicker；缓解：窗口常驻不销毁、用 CSS opacity 而非 window.show()/hide() 做展开收起。

setIgnoreMouseEvents(true,{forward:true}) 的 mousemove/mouseleave 转发不稳定：当焦点在某些非 Electron 窗口时 mousemove 可能不触发（#33281）、Windows 上 :hover/mouseleave 有 bug（#30808）、拖拽时其他窗口闪烁（#35030）。'hover 进热区展开'若纯靠 forward 的 mousemove 可能漏触发——更稳的是用一条始终可交互(不穿透)的窄边缘热区窗/区域来捕获 hover。
macOS globalShortcut 某些组合（含媒体键/系统级）需 app 被授予辅助功能(Accessibility)信任权限才能注册成功；register 在快捷键被其他 app 占用时静默失败、返回前需校验返回值。
Wayland(Linux) 下 setBounds/setSize 程序化改窗口大小可能被 WM 拒绝，展开动画在 Linux/Wayland 上需降级方案。setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces 在 Windows 上是 no-op（isVisibleOnAllWorkspaces 恒 false）。
Tauri 侧对应坑：原生 WebView 渲染差异需各平台验透明/动画；某些底层窗口能力（极端 z-order、精细 mouse-passthrough 行为）成熟度与社区案例不如 Electron 丰富，遇到平台特例可能要写 Rust 原生代码补。
apis_or_mechanisms: BrowserWindow 构造项: frame:false, transparent:true, alwaysOnTop:true, skipTaskbar:true, resizable:false, hasShadow:false, focusable
win.setAlwaysOnTop(flag, level, relativeLevel) — level:'screen-saver'/'floating'
win.setIgnoreMouseEvents(ignore, {forward:true}) — 点击穿透+事件转发
win.setBounds(bounds, true) / win.setSize(w,h,true) — animate=true 做展开动画(macOS 原生缓动)
win.setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces(true, {visibleOnFullScreen:true}) — 跨 Space 常驻(macOS/Linux)
screen.getPrimaryDisplay().workArea / getDisplayNearestPoint + 'display-metrics-changed' — 右侧贴边定位与多屏适配
app.dock.hide() / Info.plist LSUIElement — macOS 隐藏 Dock 图标
Tray (systemTray) — 常驻托盘/菜单栏入口
globalShortcut.register(accelerator, cb) — 全局唤起(返回值校验; macOS Accessibility 信任)
Tauri 2.0 对应: decorations(false), transparent, always_on_top, skip_taskbar, set_ignore_cursor_events, @tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut, tray-icon feature
feasibility.recommendation: 两者都能做到全部七项能力；如果团队已有 Web/JS 栈、看重透明/动画的跨平台渲染一致与调试便利，且能接受 ~100MB 体积/~150–300MB 常驻内存，用 Electron（动效和透明叠层更可控）；如果这个胶囊定位是'轻量、长期后台常驻、内存敏感'的小工具且团队能写 Rust，优先 Tauri 2.0（体积小一个数量级、常驻内存约低一半），但要预算各平台 WebView 的透明/动画渲染差异测试。

feasibility.question: 如何让 macOS "胶囊"窗口在所有 Space 可见、悬浮在全屏应用之上、且不抢焦点；Tauri/Electron 的 webview 窗口能否设置这些，还是必须用 native 逃生口？
findings: 核心结论：全部三项目标(全 Space 可见 + 浮于全屏之上 + 不抢焦点)用 native AppKit (NSPanel) 完全可达，这是基线;webview 框架(Tauri/Electron)能达到"全 Space 可见 + 浮于全屏",但"完全不抢焦点(non-activating)"这一项在纯 JS/config 层达不到——必须经 native 逃生口(Tauri 用 tauri-nspanel 插件或自写 objc;Electron 用 type:'panel' BaseWindow + 原生模块)才能拿到 NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel。

Native AppKit 基线(最可靠):用 NSPanel 子类,styleMask 加 .nonactivatingPanel(点击面板不激活你的 app、不抢前台 app 焦点);becomesKeyOnlyIfNeeded=true(只有点到真正需要键盘输入的文本框/控件时才成为 key window——正好匹配"hover 展开后快速写待办");level=.floating 或 .statusBar(浮于普通窗口之上,statusBar 比 floating 更高);collectionBehavior=[.canJoinAllSpaces, .fullScreenAuxiliary, .stationary]。三者缺一不可:canJoinAllSpaces 让面板跟随用户切 Space;fullScreenAuxiliary 让面板能浮在别的 app 的全屏会话之上;stationary 让面板在 Mission Control/切 Space 动画时不被当普通窗口平移。

Tauri(v2):纯 config/JS 不够。setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces / alwaysOnTop 在 dev 下偶尔像样,但 `tauri build` 产物里"浮于全屏之上"会失效或把窗口丢到非全屏桌面(已确认 bug:tauri#11488、tauri#9556、tauri#9439,均 closed as not planned——框架本身不打算修)。tauri 也没有原生 NonactivatingPanel 枚举(tao#136/#414、tauri#13034 长期未落地)。逃生口=社区插件 tauri-nspanel(ahkohd/tauri-nspanel,v2.x):把已建的 webview window 转成真正的 NSPanel,提供 PanelBuilder、PanelLevel::Floating、is_floating_panel、can_become_key_window 等;Cap、Screenpipe 等产品已用它实现"浮于全屏 + 不夺焦"。这是 Tauri 路线的推荐做法。

Electron:同样需要部分 native。setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces(true,{visibleOnFullScreen:true}) + setAlwaysOnTop(true,'screen-saver'/'floating') 能做到全 Space + 浮于全屏(参考 Daily/codestudy 实战)。但"不抢焦点"靠 BrowserWindow 选项(focusable:false 只是不可聚焦,不等于 non-activating,且会让面板内输入框收不到键盘)无法干净达成;真正的 non-activating 需要 BaseWindow 的 type:'panel'(Electron 已支持 panel 类型)或经原生模块拿 NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel。已知坑 electron#36364:setAlwaysOnTop+setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces+visibleOnFullScreen 组合在全屏上"不手动 focus 就不显示",closed as not planned。
"软件最小化后胶囊仍常驻":胶囊应是独立 NSPanel/独立窗口,与主窗口生命周期解耦——主窗口 miniaturize/close 不影响面板;面板默认不出现在 Dock/窗口菜单(NSPanel 天然如此),正好符合常驻轻量诉求。

"贴边热区 hover 展开动画":NSPanel + 鼠标跟踪可达;tauri-nspanel 内置 mouse enter/exit/move 事件,适合做边缘热区触发。展开/收起动画在 webview 内用 CSS/JS 做(transform/clip-path),窗口尺寸变化用带动画的 setFrame(display:animate:) 或保持窗口够大、内容层做动画(避免逐帧 resize 窗口卡顿,推荐后者)。
apis_or_mechanisms: NSPanel (NSWindow 子类)
NSWindowStyleMask.nonactivatingPanel (NSWindowStyleMaskNonactivatingPanel)
panel.becomesKeyOnlyIfNeeded = true
window.level = .floating / .statusBar (NSWindow.Level)
collectionBehavior = [.canJoinAllSpaces, .fullScreenAuxiliary, .stationary]
NSApp activationPolicy = .accessory (LSUIElement) — 让 app 不占 Dock,但会隐藏 Dock 图标,需权衡
Tauri: tauri-nspanel 插件 (ahkohd/tauri-nspanel v2.x) — PanelBuilder, PanelLevel::Floating, is_floating_panel, can_become_key_window, set_collection_behavior
Tauri webview API: setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces / alwaysOnTop(不足以独立达成,仅 dev 近似)
Electron: BaseWindow/BrowserWindow type:'panel'
Electron: setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces(true,{visibleOnFullScreen:true, skipTransformProcessType}), setAlwaysOnTop(true,'screen-saver'|'floating')
Electron: setIgnoreMouseEvents(true,{forward:true}) — 透传鼠标做边缘热区(可选)
setFrame(_:display:animate:) — 窗口尺寸动画(展开面板)
gotchas: webview 框架纯 config 达不到"不抢焦点":focusable:false ≠ non-activating——前者会让面板里的文本框收不到键盘(写待办失败),后者(NonactivatingPanel)才是"app 不前置但控件仍可输入"。这是本产品的关键约束,必须走 native panel 类型。
canJoinAllSpaces 单独不够浮于全屏:必须同时加 fullScreenAuxiliary,否则切到别的 app 全屏后胶囊消失。漏配是最常见错误。
Tauri 在 `tauri build` 发布产物里行为与 dev 不一致(浮于全屏失效/窗口被丢到非全屏桌面),tauri#11488/#9556/#9439 均 closed as not planned——不要指望官方修,直接上 tauri-nspanel。
Electron #36364:alwaysOnTop+visibleOnAllWorkspaces+visibleOnFullScreen 组合可能"不手动 focus 就不在全屏上显示",且官方 not planned。
activationPolicy=.accessory 能把窗口顶到系统层之上,但会从 Dock 抹掉 app 图标——如果产品要有正常 Dock 图标,别用 accessory,改用 NSPanel non-activating + collectionBehavior 达成同效果。
Electron setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces 默认会在 UIElement/Foreground 进程类型间切换,导致窗口和 Dock 短暂闪烁;若已是 UIElement 用 skipTransformProcessType:true 规避。
tao 早期把 NonactivatingPanel 枚举值直接转 cocoa 会崩溃(社区记录),所以别手搓 styleMask 位,务必用维护过的 tauri-nspanel。
动画别逐帧 resize 原生窗口(卡顿/掉帧):窗口保持展开态尺寸,内容在 webview 内用 CSS transform 动画;或用 setFrame(display:animate:) 但限制频率。
stationary 漏配会让胶囊在 Mission Control/切 Space 时被当普通窗口平移、动画割裂,影响"常驻"观感。
feasibility.recommendation: 用 NSPanel(.nonactivatingPanel + becomesKeyOnlyIfNeeded + level .statusBar + collectionBehavior [.canJoinAllSpaces,.fullScreenAuxiliary,.stationary])作为唯一可靠路径;若用 webview,选 Tauri v2 + tauri-nspanel 插件把胶囊窗口转成真 NSPanel(不抢焦/浮全屏/全 Space 全部由插件的 native 层保证),展开动画放 webview 内 CSS——不要试图只靠 Tauri/Electron 的 JS config 达成"不抢焦点",那一项在框架层达不到,必须经 native 逃生口。

feasibility.question: 刘海/胶囊类 app（NotchNook、Boring Notch、MediaMate）技术上如何实现常驻胶囊 + hover 展开？是否用原生 Swift/AppKit 无边框 NSWindow/NSPanel？对本产品"webview vs 原生"架构选择有何启示——本产品（右侧贴边常驻、置顶不抢焦点、idle 极小、hover 展开成待办面板、最小化后仍常驻、轻量美观动效好）该用 webview 还是 native Swift？
findings: 这一整类 app 全部是原生 Swift/AppKit + SwiftUI，没有一个用 webview/Electron。Boring Notch（开源，github.com/TheBoredTeam/boring.notch）自述技术栈为 SwiftUI + Core Animation + Combine + 私有 MediaRemote framework；DynamicNotchKit（github.com/MrKai77/DynamicNotchKit，被多个 notch app 复用的库）明确 'all the UI is handled by SwiftUI'，底层自己 'drawing a custom window'（即自定义 NSPanel）。NotchNook 是闭源商业 app（lo.cafe，$25 买断/$3 月订），但行为特征（贴 notch、非抢焦点、media live activity）与开源同类完全一致，几乎确定同构。

核心窗口原语是 NSPanel（NSWindow 的子类，专为辅助/浮动窗口设计），不是普通 NSWindow。关键配置：styleMask 含 .borderless（无标题栏边框，'displays none of the usual peripheral elements'）+ .nonactivatingPanel（点击面板不把 app 拉到前台、不抢焦点——这正是'置顶不抢焦点'的核心 API）。

置顶用 window.level 设为高层级——浮动面板用 .floating，需要盖过菜单栏/全屏的 notch app 通常用更高的 .statusBar 或 .screenSaver（NSScreenSaverWindowLevel）。跨 Space / 全屏常驻用 collectionBehavior：.canJoinAllSpaces（跟随用户在所有 Space 显示）+ .fullScreenAuxiliary（在别的 app 全屏时仍可叠加显示）+ .stationary。

hover 展开靠 AppKit 的 NSTrackingArea：在贴边/notch 区域注册一个 tracking rect，options 设 [.mouseEnteredAndExited, .activeAlways, .inVisibleRect]。关键是 .activeAlways（而非 .activeInKeyWindow）——这样即使 app 不在前台、面板没焦点，鼠标进入热区也能收到 mouseEntered 回调触发展开。SwiftUI 侧也可用 .onHover，但贴边热区检测更可靠的是 AppKit tracking area。

展开动画：idle 小胶囊 ↔ 展开面板的形变/尺寸过渡用 Core Animation / SwiftUI 隐式动画（.spring/.easeInOut）。做法是面板窗口本身保持一个较大的固定 frame，内容用 SwiftUI matchedGeometry / 尺寸动画在窗口内伸缩；或动态改 NSPanel 的 setFrame(display:animate:)。Boring Notch 明确用 Core Animation 做 smooth transitions。

'最小化后胶囊仍常驻' = 把 app 设为 accessory/agent 类型：Info.plist 设 LSUIElement=1，或运行时 NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)。这样 app 无 Dock 图标、无主菜单栏，但浮动 NSPanel 持续显示，主窗口关闭/'最小化'不影响胶囊存活。胶囊本质是一个常驻 agent 进程托管的 NSPanel，不是某个主窗口的子视图。
成熟可复用基建已存在：DynamicNotchKit / DynamicNotch / mew-notch / Atoll 等开源库已封装'自定义贴边窗口 + 安全区 + 动画 + hover'，本产品（右侧胶囊，不一定贴 notch）可直接借鉴其 NSPanel 配置代码，不必从零写。

webview 路线（Electron / Tauri / WKWebView 套壳）在本场景有硬伤：Electron 难以做到真正的 .nonactivatingPanel 非抢焦点 + .canJoinAllSpaces 全 Space 常驻 + 高 window level 盖菜单栏，BrowserWindow 的 alwaysOnTop/level 映射粗糙且不暴露 NSTrackingArea 级别的边缘热区；且单胶囊 app 背一个 ~100-150MB Chromium 运行时与'轻量'诉求直接冲突。
gotchas: LSUIElement/accessory 模式下 macOS 不允许'有主菜单栏但无 Dock 图标'——要么都有要么都没有。agent app 通常无 Dock 无主菜单栏，设置入口靠 menu bar status item 或胶囊内的按钮，不要指望传统 App 菜单。
.nonactivatingPanel 面板默认 canBecomeKey/canBecomeMain 为 false，文本输入（写待办的输入框）拿不到键盘焦点。必须子类化 NSPanel 重写 canBecomeKey 返回 true（同时保留 nonactivating 不抢 app 焦点），否则 hover 展开后输入框打不了字——这是该交互最常见的坑。
高 window level（.screenSaver/.statusBar）会盖住几乎所有东西，包括别的 app 的弹窗甚至部分系统 UI；level 调过高体验割裂，需精调到刚好盖住目标层级。
collectionBehavior 若不设 .canJoinAllSpaces，胶囊只在创建它的那个 Space 显示，切 Space 就消失——'常驻'诉求必须显式加这个 flag。.fullScreenAuxiliary 不加则别的 app 进全屏时胶囊被遮。
NSTrackingArea 在多显示器 / 显示器热插拔 / 分辨率变化时 rect 坐标会失效，需监听 NSApplication.didChangeScreenParametersNotification 重建 tracking area 和重定位面板。贴边坐标要从 NSScreen.visibleFrame 实时算，别硬编码。
全屏 app 之上叠加常驻浮层在新版 macOS 上对非 notch 区域有额外限制（系统对覆盖菜单栏/全屏的浮层管控趋严），需在目标 macOS 版本实测；这也是为什么这类 app 多绑定 notch 区（系统对 notch 周边相对宽容）。
Boring Notch 用了私有 MediaRemote framework——那是为媒体控制功能，本产品做待办面板不需要私有 API，纯公开 AppKit 即可，不要被'它们用私有 framework'误导成本产品也必须用。
若坚持 webview 取美观/动效，折中是原生 Swift 写 NSPanel + hover + 动画外壳，面板内容嵌 WKWebView 渲染待办 UI——但这样仍需写全套 AppKit 窗口代码，省不掉原生工作量，只是把内容层换成 web，收益有限且增加 IPC/性能复杂度。
feasibility.recommendation: 用原生 Swift（AppKit NSPanel + SwiftUI 内容 + NSTrackingArea hover + accessory 模式），直接参照 DynamicNotchKit 等开源库的窗口配置；webview/Electron 在'非抢焦点 + 全 Space 常驻 + 边缘热区 + 轻量'这四点上做不到或代价过高，不建议。
apis_or_mechanisms: NSPanel（NSWindow 子类，浮动窗口原语）
styleMask: .borderless + .nonactivatingPanel
NSWindow.level: .floating / .statusBar / .screenSaver(NSScreenSaverWindowLevel)
collectionBehavior: .canJoinAllSpaces, .fullScreenAuxiliary, .stationary
重写 NSPanel.canBecomeKey/canBecomeMain → true（让输入框可输入而不抢 app 焦点）
NSTrackingArea(options: [.mouseEnteredAndExited, .activeAlways, .inVisibleRect]) → mouseEntered/mouseExited
NSHostingView（把 SwiftUI 视图嵌入 NSPanel）
NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory) / Info.plist LSUIElement=1（无 Dock 常驻 agent）
Core Animation / SwiftUI .spring 隐式动画 + NSPanel.setFrame(_:display:animate:)（胶囊↔面板形变）
NSApplication.didChangeScreenParametersNotification（多屏/分辨率变化重建热区）
NSScreen.visibleFrame（贴边坐标实时计算）
SwiftUI .onHover（轻量 hover，贴边热区仍优先 NSTrackingArea）

feasibility.question: macOS engineering reality for an independent small utility: global hotkey capture, lightweight local persistence (SQLite/JSON), launch-at-login, menu bar item, and packaging/notarization — for an always-on, edge-docked, topmost-but-non-focus-stealing "capsule" window that idles tiny and animates open into a todo panel on edge-hover hot-zone, persisting after minimize.
findings: GLOBAL HOTKEY: Fully feasible with mature, MAS-compatible tooling. The de-facto choice is sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts (SPM), which gives user-customizable, recordable shortcuts via a SwiftUI Recorder component and is explicitly sandbox + Mac App Store compatible. Under the hood global hotkeys still rely on Carbon's RegisterEventHotKey (or a CGEventTap); Apple never shipped a modern replacement, but the deprecated Carbon path remains the supported reality and Apple states new APIs will land before removal. soffes/HotKey is the lighter wrapper if you only need hard-coded keys with no settings UI.

NON-FOCUS-STEALING TOPMOST WINDOW: This is the single most important architectural decision and it is feasible — use NSPanel (not NSWindow) with styleMask .nonactivatingPanel. A nonactivating panel can become KEY (receive keyboard input, so the todo text field is editable) WITHOUT becoming MAIN and WITHOUT activating/foregrounding your app, so the user's current app keeps focus. Set window.level = .floating (or .statusBar) for always-on-top, collectionBehavior = [.canJoinAllSpaces, .fullScreenAuxiliary] so the capsule rides over every Space and over other apps' full-screen windows. Subclass NSPanel and override canBecomeKey = true.

EDGE-HOVER REVEAL: The capsule idling tiny means the cursor is OUTSIDE the window most of the time, so an in-window NSTrackingArea/mouseEntered is insufficient for the initial trigger. Use NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents(matching: [.mouseMoved]) to watch the cursor globally; when X is within an edge threshold (e.g. within ~4px of the right screen edge AND inside the capsule's Y-band), animate expansion. Crucially: a GLOBAL mouse-MOVED monitor does NOT require Accessibility permission (only synthetic event POSTING or key monitoring does), so the hot-zone works permission-free. Once expanded, switch to in-window NSTrackingArea (.mouseEnteredAndExited + .activeAlways) to detect mouseExited and collapse.

EXPAND/COLLAPSE ANIMATION: Animate by resizing the panel frame: window.animator().setFrame(expandedRect, display: true) inside NSAnimationContext.runAnimationGroup with a custom timingFunction, or animate SwiftUI content inside a fixed max-size panel with .animation/withAnimation (often smoother — keep the panel at full expanded size, animate internal content offset/opacity, so you avoid per-frame window resize cost). Pin the panel to the screen's right edge by computing frame from NSScreen.visibleFrame. 'Stays after minimize' is automatic: the panel is independent of any main window; if you also have a main window, minimizing/closing it does not affect the panel as long as the app process keeps running (LSUIElement keeps it running with no Dock presence).

MENU BAR ITEM: Feasible and standard. Two routes: (1) SwiftUI MenuBarExtra (macOS 13+) with .menuBarExtraStyle(.window) — simplest, reactive; but the .window popover is size-constrained (~half screen) and less positionally flexible.
